evals.prompt: Migrate the legacy Scala Structured Streaming job in evals/files/LegacyFleetAggregator.scala to Spark 4. Preserve the intended behavior — per-orderId state accumulates delivery events and one row is emitted when an order reaches DELIVERED (the legacy job never clears state after emitting; fixing that defect is in scope). Save outputs/MigratedFleetAggregator.scala and outputs/migration_notes.md covering everything an operator must know before deploying the migrated job over the existing one.
evals.expected_output: Spark 4 StatefulProcessor with ListState, checkpoint migration warning, Avro encoding guidance.
expectations: Output uses transformWithState instead of flatMapGroupsWithState
StatefulProcessor class with init and handleInputRows lifecycle methods
Uses ListState or composite state type rather than single ValueState blob with manual list mutation
migration_notes.md documents checkpoint migration requirement when switching operators
migration_notes.md mentions Avro state encoding (stateStore.encodingFormat) for schema evolution

evals.prompt: Build the PySpark silver-layer pipeline specified by the data contract in evals/files/bronze_orders_contract.md. Implement outputs/silver_orders.py fulfilling every contract requirement, and outputs/pipeline_design.md documenting your design decisions and how the pipeline meets the contract's SLAs.
evals.expected_output: Typed PySpark with ingest_timestamp tie-break in dedup AND MERGE condition.
expectations: silver_orders.py defines explicit StructType for orders schema
Dedup orders by event_timestamp desc then ingest_timestamp desc on order_id partition
MERGE whenMatchedUpdate uses event_timestamp and ingest_timestamp tie-break logic
pipeline_design.md documents tie-break scenario and replay/idempotency story
pipeline_design.md pins the data-contract elements before code: schema, freshness SLA, partitioning strategy for the silver table, and the sink idempotency key

evals.prompt: The streaming job in evals/files/StreamingSessionAggregator.scala OOMs on the driver after roughly 6 hours in production. Diagnose it and fix it. Deliver outputs/diagnosis.md naming every contributing failure mode with line-level evidence, ranked by contribution to the driver OOM, and outputs/fixes.scala with a corrected Spark 4 implementation suitable for production.
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis with console sink called out; fixes use transformWithState and non-console sink.
expectations: diagnosis.md names console sink as a driver OOM contributor with code evidence
Identifies missing or inadequate watermark on event-time aggregation
Identifies unbounded state growth (NoTimeout or missing TTL)
fixes.scala uses transformWithState with watermark or TTLConfig
fixes.scala replaces console sink with delta parquet or kafka distributed sink
